entries.text_ar: And among mankind there is the one who worships God, as it were, on a knife edge๏ฟฝThe believer has one face, without a reverse side; he makes repeated [advances] and never retreats. You will see him striving for the cause of God๏ฟฝs religion and His obedience, upholding God๏ฟฝs oneness and the emulation of His Prophet , constantly making humble entreaty (taแธarruสฟ) of God and seeking refuge in Him in the hope of connecting to Him through following [exemplaryguidance]. Zayd b. Aslam related from the Prophet that he said: ๏ฟฝEvery person of my nation (umma) will enter Paradise, save the one who refuses.๏ฟฝ We said, ๏ฟฝO Messenger of God who will refuse that?๏ฟฝ He said, ๏ฟฝWhoever obeys me will enter Paradise, and whoever disobeys me has refused to enter Paradise.๏ฟฝ His words, Exalted is He:๏ฟฝif good [fortune] befalls him, he is reassured by it๏ฟฝThat is, whoever follows [his] desire, if his heart is satisfied and his lower self is happy in its worldly share, he will feel reassured by it. Otherwise he will return to the disbelief that his desire calls him to. His words:

Truly God shall admit those who believe and perform righteous deeds into gardens๏ฟฝHe said:They are those who were faithful to God in secret as well as in public, and followed the Sunna of the Prophet and did not innovate in any situation.His words:

๏ฟฝthe sun and the moon... He said:The prostration of these things consists of their recognition (maสฟrifa) of God [manifested] through their abasing themsleves (tadhallul) and submitting (inqiyฤd) to Him.His words:

๏ฟฝPurify My House for those who circumambulate it๏ฟฝThat is, ๏ฟฝPurify My House from idols, for those among My servants whose hearts are pure from doubt (shakk), misgivings (rayb) and hardness (qaswa). Therefore, just as God has commandedthe purification of His House [at Mecca] from idols, so also He has commanded the purification of that house of His in which He deposited the mystery of faith (sirr al-ฤซmฤn) and the light of gnosis (nลซr al-maสฟrifa), namely, the heart of the believer. God, Exalted is He, has commanded the believer to purify himself from rancour (ghill), deceit (ghishsh), inclination towards the lusts of the lower self (shahawฤt al-nafs) and heedlessness (ghafla). For those who circumambulate it [the Kaสฟba], as also for those who uphold the lights of faith, there will be manifold increase in [God-given] success (tawfฤซq).๏ฟฝAnd those who bow and prostrate themselvesin fear and hope. Truly the heart โธขis [like] a houseโธฃ: if it is unoccupied it goes to ruin, and [likewise] if it is occupied by other than its owner, โธขor by other than one whom the owner has settled thereโธฃ, it will also go to ruin. Therefore, if you wish your hearts to be in good repair, do not let your prayer in them be other than to God, Exalted is He; โธขif you want to keep your breasts (แนฃuแธลซr) in good repair, let not anything be in them other than [awareness of] the Hereafterโธฃ; if you want to preserve your tongues, do not let your prayer on them be other than truthfulness (แนฃidq); and if you wish to preserve your bodily members, do not allow them to become engaged with anything other than what is in accordance with the Sunna.His words:

And announce among the people the [season for] Pilgrimage. They shall come to you on foot๏ฟฝVerily God, Exalted is He, has servants among whom some go to mosques on thrones (sarฤซr), and some ride on conveyances of gold covered with silk and drawn by angels.Aแธฅmad b. Sฤlim said, ๏ฟฝI was working on a piece of land to improve it, when I saw Sahl on a couch above the water of the Euphrates River.๏ฟฝ He also said, ๏ฟฝOne day I entered Sahl๏ฟฝs house, the door of which was very small, and I saw a horse standing there. Then I left in fright, wondering how it could have entered through such a small door. Sahl saw me and told me to come back, so I returned and saw nothing there.๏ฟฝ It has been related that สฟUmar b. al-Khaแนญแนญฤb was gazing down on the congregation at สฟArafฤt and said, ๏ฟฝIf only the congregation in this plain knew [the hosts] who have descended, they would have rejoiced at His bounty (faแธl) after [receiving tidings of] being forgiven (maghfira)๏ฟฝ. His words:

๏ฟฝAnd celebrate God๏ฟฝs name, on specified days, over the livestock He has provided for them๏ฟฝ[By which] is meant the gifts and sacrifices. It was related of Fatแธฅ al-Mawแนฃilฤซ that on the day of Eid he looked down over the city of Mosul and could see smoke rising from many houses, so he said, ๏ฟฝO my Lord! How many people are drawing closer to You through sacrifice (qurbฤn) this night! I have also tried to draw closer to You through sacrifice,๏ฟฝ that is to say, through prayers (แนฃalawฤt). ๏ฟฝWhat will You make of it, O Beloved One?๏ฟฝ It was related of สฟAdฤซ b. Thฤbital-Anแนฃฤrฤซ that he said, ๏ฟฝThe sacrifice of those who are mindful of God (muttaqลซn) is prayer.๏ฟฝ But God knows best.His words:

๏ฟฝAnd perform the circumambulation of the Ancient House.He said:People have differed concerning this. แธคasan said, ๏ฟฝHe called it Ancient (สฟatฤซq) to honour it, just as the Arabs speak of an ๏ฟฝancient๏ฟฝ body, and an ๏ฟฝancient๏ฟฝ horse if it is noble.๏ฟฝ My maternal uncle, Muแธฅammad b. Sawwฤr, related on the authority of al-Thawrฤซ that he said, ๏ฟฝIt is called this because it is the oldest (aqdam) and most ancient (aสฟtaq) place of worship belonging to God, just as He said: The first House (of worship) to be established for people was that at Bakka, a blessed place [3:96].๏ฟฝSome have said, ๏ฟฝHe [God] called it สฟatฤซq because no tyrant ever headed for it with an evil design without God๏ฟฝs destroying him and freeing (aสฟtaqa) His House from him. Some have said, ๏ฟฝIt is because it was saved (uสฟtiqa) from being submerged during the great flood and raised to the sky.๏ฟฝ Just as God has freed His House, so He has also freed the heart of the believer from other [than Him]. It [the Kaสฟba] is older than anything else that God, Exalted is He, erected on His earth as a sign (สฟalam), and He placed it within the Sacred Mosque (al-Masjid al-แธคarฤm). Likewise the heart has [within it] another heart, which is the position from which the servant stands before his Master without being agitated or busied by anything, but in a state of tranquil repose in Him (sฤkin ilayhi).His words, Exalted is He:

And among mankind are those who dispute about God without any knowledge๏ฟฝThat is, they dispute about religion as their whim (hawฤ) [dictates], or through analogical [reasoning] (qiyฤs), without following [exemplary guidance] (iqtidฤสพ). By doing this they lead people astray and produce innovation.His words, Exalted is He:

๏ฟฝIndeed, it is not the eyes that become blind, but it is their hearts that become blind within the breasts.He said:Is it not true that with the light of the heart๏ฟฝs insight (nลซr baแนฃar al-qalb) a person can overcome desire and lust? But when the heart๏ฟฝs sight is blind to what is within it, lust will overcome him and heedlessness (ghafla) will [afflict] him at regular intervals. Consequently his body will stray into sin without being guided to God under any circumstances.His words, Exalted is He:

We did not send before you any Messenger or prophet but that when he recited [the scripture]Satan cast [something] into his recitation๏ฟฝHe said:That is, if he recites [the Scripture] and his lower self notices the recitation, Satan casts [somethinginto it], for he has a partnership with the lower self in this, and that noticing of the recitation comes from the desire of the lower self and its lust. However, if [the person reciting] then witnesses the One remembered (madhkลซr) instead of the remembrance [itself] (dhikr), the heart will become oblivious of it [the recitation], and he will not witness anything besides his Master. Consequently Satan will become one of his captives. Do you not see how if a servant is inattentive (sahฤ) in his recitation (qirฤสพa) or in his remembrance of his Lord, Mighty andMajestic is He, his heart acquiesces in the basest pleasures (huแบลซแบ) of his lower self and thus can the Enemy [Satan] get at him. แธคasan said, ๏ฟฝThe whispering (waswฤs) is of two kinds, one of which is from the lower self and the other from Satan. That which persists in coming is from the lower self, and assistance against it is sought through fasting, prayer and propriety (adab). However, that which [comes, but then] withdraws (nabdhan) is from Satan, and assistance against it is sought through the recitation of the Qurสพฤn and remembrance [of God] (dhikr).๏ฟฝHis words:

๏ฟฝso that they may believe therein and their hearts may be humbled to Him๏ฟฝHe said:True faith (แนฃidq al-ฤซmฤn) and its [true] realisation (แธฅaqฤซqa) produce humility (ikhbฤt) within the heart, which consists of tenderness (riqqa), fear (khashiya) and humble submission (khushลซสฟ) in the heart, and results in long periods of reflection (fikr) and silence (แนฃamt). These are among the results of faith, for God, Exalted is He, has said: So that they may believe in it and their hearts may be humbled to Him. And God knows best.His words, Exalted is He:
